query: WGA 2026 strike duration seven weeks start end dates timeline

{"query":"WGA 2026 strike duration seven weeks start end dates timeline","model":"mako","response_time_ms":11349,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.wga.org/contracts/contracts/mba/summary-of-the-2026-wga-mba","title":"Summary of the 2026 WGA MBA","content":"In this agreement, we negotiated increased health contribution rates and caps along with diverting monies from the Paid Parental Leave Fund.
As an alternative to the Anthem PPO, starting in 2027 the Health Fund will offer a new plan option for active participants and pre-Medicare retirees through a network called Centivo, which includes all of the UCLA health system in Los Angeles and Mount Sinai in NY as well as providers in other parts of the country.
Additionally, Extended Coverage under the Anthem PPO plan will cost 4 points per quarter, while Extended Coverage under the new Centivo plan option will cost 2.5 points per quarter.
The contract increases the annual funding for the Showrunner Training Program from $250,000 to $275,000, which will support both WGAW and WGAE’s programs."},{"url":"https://www.wgacontract.org/","title":"WGA Contract 2026","content":"This FAQ addresses key provisions in the 2026 MBA Memorandum of Agreement.
The WGA membership has ratified the 2026 MBA, with 90.38% (4,282) voting in favor and 9.62% (456) voting no.
The 2026 MBA Negotiating Committee, WGAW Board of Directors and WGAE Council have voted unanimously to recommend the tentative agreement to both Guilds’ memberships for a ratification vote.
Read the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)."},{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/news/wga-staff-strike-weeks-ahead-amptp-negotiations-1236665769/","title":"WGA Staff Go on Strike Weeks Ahead of AMPTP Negotiations","content":"The staff of the Writers Guild of America West went on strike on Tuesday, accusing leadership of the union of breaking labor laws.
Members of the staff union began picketing outside WGA West headquarters on Tuesday afternoon, carrying signs like “It’s Giving AMPTP” and “WGA Live Your Labor Values.” Scabby the Rat, a large inflatable mascot that is a familiar sight on picket lines, was parked at 3rd Street and Fairfax Avenue.
“During the course of 19 negotiating sessions since September, the Guild has offered the staff union comprehensive proposals with numerous union protections and improvements to compensation and working conditions,” the WGA West said.
The WGAW Board, WGA Negotiating Committee and executive staff will continue to prepare for the upcoming MBA negotiations, and management staff will carry on the core functions of the Guild.
The union organized last spring, and negotiations on its first contract began last September."},{"url":"https://deadline.com/2026/03/wga-west-staff-strike-ending-updated-contract-proposal-1236767163/","title":"WGA West Staff Delivers Updated Contract Proposal To Management","content":"The WGSU accused leadership of having “chosen a war path with its own employees” that has led the strike to collide with the writers’ negotiations with the Hollywood studios.
The WGA West did not immediately respond to Deadline’s request for comment.
The WGA West staff union is represented by the Pacific Northwest Staff Union.
It includes about 115 of the guild’s 160 employees and encompasses residuals & dues processors, IT & data management workers, organizers, communications specialists, legal personnel, researchers, Writers Guild Theater employees, contract enforcement staff, and more.
Prior to this week, the staff had been dutifully marching outside WGAW headquarters on the busy intersection of 3rd St. & Fairfax Ave. in Los Angeles for a full month."},{"url":"https://www.wga.org/uploadedfiles/contracts/2026_mba_moa.pdf","title":"2026 WGA MOA","content":"Apr 4, 2026 — Motion Picture Agreement shall commence on September 25, 2023 May 2, 2026 and shall continue to and include May 1, 2026 2030.46 pages"}]}

May 8, 2026 ; Writers Guild members ratify new contract with","score":0.6937432,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/news/wga-staff-strike-weeks-ahead-amptp-negotiations-1236665769","title":"WGA Staff Go on Strike Weeks Ahead of AMPTP Negotiations","content":"# WGA Staff Go on Strike Weeks Ahead of AMPTP Negotiations. Protestors at the Strike by the WGA Staff Ahead of AMPTP Negotiations on February 17, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. The staff of the Writers Guild of America West went on strike on Tuesday, accusing leadership of the union of breaking labor laws. “The Writers Guild Staff Union (WGSU) has called an unfair labor practice strike in protest of ULPs committed by Writers Guild of America West management,” the union said in a statement. “Guild management has surveilled workers for union activity, terminated union supporters, and engaged in bad faith surface bargaining, showing no intention to come to an agreement on most of WGSU’s core issues.”. In a statement on the guild’s website, the WGA said that it will continue to bargain in good faith with the staff. The WGA West has denied the allegations of bad faith bargaining, but said in a statement that it respects the staff’s right to strike.","score":0.67063034,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://onlabor.org/april-7-2026","title":"News & Commentary: April 7, 2026 ✦ OnLabor","content":"In today’s News and Commentary, the WGA reaches a deal with studios, a strike at meatpacking company JBS USA brings the employer back to the table, and union leaders gear up to take on AI. However, the good news is paired with internal strife at the WGA, as the Writers Guild Staff Union has been on strike since February 17, with no clear end in sight. In Colorado, multinational meatpacking company JBS USA has agreed to go back to the negotiating table with United Food and Commercial Workers Local 7 following a 3-week unfair labor practice strike. In today’s News and Commentary, student-athletes file a class action suit challenging the NCAA’s new Age-Based Rule, a federal judge declines to issue a preliminary injunction against FEMA’s reduction in force but expedites proceedings, and Gavin Newsom opposes California’s proposed billionaire tax in favor of a federal approach. ## OnLabor In The News.","score":0.38512084,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.etonline.com/sag-and-wga-strikes-all-the-major-dates-to-know-207915","title":"SAG-AFTRA and WGA Strikes: All the Major Dates to Know | Entertainment Tonight","content":"## This summer's work stoppage marked the first time in six decades Hollywood's actors and writers went on strike simultaneously. The Screen Actors Guild announced its deal to end their 118-day strike in November, drawing to a close a period of labor negotiations that practically grounded Hollywood to a stop for nearly six months. Both the Writers Guild of America (WGA) and the Screen Actors Guild and American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG-AFTRA) remained on picket lines for well over 100 days while stalled in their negotiations with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers (AMPTP), which represents the studios and streamers that produce a vast majority of filmed content. The dual work stoppage of both unions this summer marked the first time in 63 years that Hollywood's writers and actors joined in a strike together. Read on for ET's complete timeline on the WGA and SAG-AFTRA's negotiations with AMPTP.","score":0.30975452,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2023_Writers_Guild_of_America_strike","title":"2023 Writers Guild of America strike","content":"The picket sign in the center references the television series Severance, the production of which was shut down due to the strike. | Caused by | * Lack of agreement on a new contract between the WGA and AMPTP * Poor compensation for writers via residuals \"Residual (entertainment industry)\") with the advent of streaming media |. | |  |  | | --- | --- | | Writers Guild of America   * Writers Guild of America West * Writers Guild of America, East | Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers | |. From May 2 to September 27, 2023, the Writers Guild of America (WGA)—representing 11,500 screenwriters—went on strike over a labor dispute with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers (AMPTP). On April 18, 2023, 97.85% of members of the Writers Guild of America (WGA) voted to go on strike if they failed to reach a satisfactory agreement with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers (AMPTP), which represents the major film and television studios \"Big Three (American television)\") in Hollywood, by May 1.","score":0.24258713,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.99,"request_id":"e6f82501-ded6-4df0-9509-9d3a32daaa1d"}
